revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Maruhakayama Kofun | native_name = 丸墓山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Maruhakayama Tumulus In Spring 2.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|45.51|N|139|28|42.62|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Maruhakayama Kofun'''|丸墓山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Maruhakayama Kofun is a circular-type ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}) ''kofun'' with a diameter of 105 meters and height of 18.9 meters, and is thus one of the largest circular tumuli in Japan.ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> [[Archaeological excavation]]s have not been conducted and details of the [[burial chamber]] are unknown. The surface of the tumulus appears to have been covered only in ''[[fukiishi]]'', with no ''[[haniwa]]'' shards having been discovered, and from this it is estimated that the tumulus was built in the early 6th century AD. From 1985 to 1986, maintenance work was conducted on the top and east side of the mound, and on a portion of the moat.
In 1590, during [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s [[Siege of Odawara (1590)|campaign against the Odawara Hōjō]], [[Ishida Mitsunari]] established his field headquarters on top of this tumulus, constructing a semi-circular embankment as part of the [[Siege of Oshi]] Castle, which is just visible from the top of the mound.
<gallery widths="140px"> Maruhakayama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|Panoramic view Gyoda Ishida Bank In Sakitama Kofun Park 1.JPG|Ishida Embankment Gyoda Maruhakayama Tumulus In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sakitama Fire Festival </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline}} *[https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/15/04/12/map/tetu.html Gyoda city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/埼玉古墳群紹介/丸墓山古墳 Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Burial Mounds] {{in lang|ja}}
[[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[Category:History of Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Gyōda]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]]

From 1985 to 1986, maintenance work was conducted on the top and east side of the mound, and on a portion of the moat.
In 1590, during [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s [[Siege of Odawara (1590)|campaign against the Odawara Hōjō]], [[Ishida Mitsunari]] established his field headquarters on top of this tumulus, constructing a semi-circular embankment as part of the [[Siege of Oshi]] Castle, which is just visible from the top of the mound.
<gallery widths="140px"> Maruhakayama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|Panoramic view Gyoda Ishida Bank In Sakitama Kofun Park 1.JPG|Ishida Embankment Gyoda Maruhakayama Tumulus In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sakitama Fire Festival </gallery>

In 1590, during [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s [[Siege of Odawara (1590)|campaign against the Odawara Hōjō]], [[Ishida Mitsunari]] established his field headquarters on top of this tumulus, constructing a semi-circular embankment as part of the [[Siege of Oshi]] Castle, which is just visible from the top of the mound.

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Gyōda, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Maruhakayama Kofun | native_name = 丸墓山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Maruhakayama Tumulus In Spring from close.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|45.51|N|139|28|42.62|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Maruhakayama Kofun'''|丸墓山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Maruhakayama Kofun is a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}) ''kofun'' with a diameter of 105 meters and height of 18.9 meters, and is thus one of the largest circular tumuli in Japan.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> [[Archaeological excavation]]s have not been conducted and details of the [[burial chamber]] are unknown. The surface of the tumulus appears to have been covered only in ''[[fukiishi]]'', with no ''[[haniwa]]'' shards having been discovered, and from this it is estimated that the tumulus was built in the early 6th century AD. From 1985 to 1986, maintenance work was conducted on the top and east side of the mound, and on a portion of the moat.
In 1590, during [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s [[Siege of Odawara (1590)|campaign against the Odawara Hōjō]], [[Ishida Mitsunari]] established his field headquarters on top of this tumulus, constructing a semi-circular embankment as part of the [[Siege of Oshi]] Castle, which is just visible from the top of the mound.
<gallery widths="140px"> Maruhakayama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|Panoramic view Gyoda Ishida Bank In Sakitama Kofun Park 1.JPG|Ishida Embankment Gyoda Maruhakayama Tumulus In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sakitama Fire Festival Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival in 2018 </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]

revision.comment: Bumping for adding the Kofun template
revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Gyōda, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Maruhakayama Kofun | native_name = 丸墓山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Maruhakayama Tumulus In Spring from close.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|45.51|N|139|28|42.62|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Maruhakayama Kofun'''|丸墓山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Maruhakayama Kofun is a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}) ''kofun'' with a diameter of 105 meters and height of 18.9 meters, and is thus one of the largest circular tumuli in Japan.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> [[Archaeological excavation]]s have not been conducted and details of the [[burial chamber]] are unknown. The surface of the tumulus appears to have been covered only in ''[[fukiishi]]'', with no ''[[haniwa]]'' shards having been discovered, and from this it is estimated that the tumulus was built in the early 6th century AD. From 1985 to 1986, maintenance work was conducted on the top and east side of the mound, and on a portion of the moat.
In 1590, during [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s [[Siege of Odawara (1590)|campaign against the Odawara Hōjō]], [[Ishida Mitsunari]] established his field headquarters on top of this tumulus, constructing a semi-circular embankment as part of the [[Siege of Oshi]] Castle, which is just visible from the top of the mound.
<gallery widths="140px"> Maruhakayama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|Panoramic view Gyoda Ishida Bank In Sakitama Kofun Park 1.JPG|Ishida Embankment Gyoda Maruhakayama Tumulus In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sakitama Fire Festival Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival in 2018 </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

The surface of the tumulus appears to have been covered only in ''[[fukiishi]]'', with no ''[[haniwa]]'' shards having been discovered, and from this it is estimated that the tumulus was built in the early 6th century AD. From 1985 to 1986, maintenance work was conducted on the top and east side of the mound, and on a portion of the moat.
In 1590, during [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s [[Siege of Odawara (1590)|campaign against the Odawara Hōjō]], [[Ishida Mitsunari]] established his field headquarters on top of this tumulus, constructing a semi-circular embankment as part of the [[Siege of Oshi]] Castle, which is just visible from the top of the mound.

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Gyōda, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Maruhakayama Kofun | native_name = 丸墓山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Maruhakayama Tumulus In Spring from close.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|45.51|N|139|28|42.62|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Maruhakayama Kofun'''|丸墓山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Maruhakayama Kofun is a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}) ''kofun'' with a diameter of 105 meters and height of 18.9 meters, and is thus one of the largest circular tumuli in Japan.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> [[Archaeological excavation]]s have not been conducted and details of the [[burial chamber]] are unknown. The surface of the tumulus appears to have been covered only in ''[[fukiishi]]'', with no ''[[haniwa]]'' shards having been discovered, and from this it is estimated that the tumulus was built in the early 6th century AD. From 1985 to 1986, maintenance work was conducted on the top and east side of the mound, and on a portion of the moat.
In 1590, during [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s [[Siege of Odawara (1590)|campaign against the Odawara Hōjō]], [[Ishida Mitsunari]] established his field headquarters on top of this tumulus, constructing a semi-circular embankment as part of the [[Siege of Oshi]] Castle, which is just visible from the top of the mound.
<gallery widths="140px"> Maruhakayama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|Panoramic view Gyoda Ishida Bank In Sakitama Kofun Park 1.JPG|Ishida Embankment Gyoda Maruhakayama Tumulus In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sakitama Fire Festival Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival in 2018 </gallery>

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Gyōda, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Maruhakayama Kofun | native_name = 丸墓山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Maruhakayama Tumulus In Spring from close.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|45.51|N|139|28|42.62|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Maruhakayama Kofun'''|丸墓山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Maruhakayama Kofun is a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}) ''kofun'' with a diameter of 105 meters and height of 18.9 meters, and is thus one of the largest circular tumuli in Japan.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> [[Archaeological excavation]]s have not been conducted and details of the [[burial chamber]] are unknown. The surface of the tumulus appears to have been covered only in ''[[fukiishi]]'', with no ''[[haniwa]]'' shards having been discovered, and from this it is estimated that the tumulus was built in the early 6th century AD. From 1985 to 1986, maintenance work was conducted on the top and east side of the mound, and on a portion of the moat.
In 1590, during [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s [[Siege of Odawara (1590)|campaign against the Odawara Hōjō]], [[Ishida Mitsunari]] established his field headquarters on top of this tumulus, constructing a semi-circular embankment as part of the [[Siege of Oshi]] Castle, which is just visible from the top of the mound.
<gallery widths="140px"> Maruhakayama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|Panoramic view Gyoda Ishida Bank In Sakitama Kofun Park 1.JPG|Ishida Embankment Gyoda Maruhakayama Tumulus In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sakitama Fire Festival Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival in 2018 </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Gyōda, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Maruhakayama Kofun | native_name = 丸墓山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Maruhakayama Tumulus In Spring from close.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|45.51|N|139|28|42.62|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Maruhakayama Kofun'''|丸墓山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Maruhakayama Kofun is a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}) ''kofun'' with a diameter of 105 meters and height of 18.9 meters, and is thus one of the largest circular tumuli in Japan.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> [[Archaeological excavation]]s have not been conducted and details of the [[burial chamber]] are unknown. The surface of the tumulus appears to have been covered only in ''[[fukiishi]]'', with no ''[[haniwa]]'' shards having been discovered, and from this it is estimated that the tumulus was built in the early 6th century AD. From 1985 to 1986, maintenance work was conducted on the top and east side of the mound, and on a portion of the moat.
In 1590, during [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s [[Siege of Odawara (1590)|campaign against the Odawara Hōjō]], [[Ishida Mitsunari]] established his field headquarters on top of this tumulus, constructing a semi-circular embankment as part of the [[Siege of Oshi]] Castle, which is just visible from the top of the mound.
<gallery widths="140px"> Maruhakayama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|Panoramic view Gyoda Ishida Bank In Sakitama Kofun Park 1.JPG|Ishida Embankment Gyoda Maruhakayama Tumulus In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sakitama Fire Festival Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival in 2018 </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]] to [[Category:qq-{{subst:NAMESPACE}}-qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Gyōda, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Maruhakayama Kofun | native_name = 丸墓山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Maruhakayama Tumulus In Spring from close.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|45.51|N|139|28|42.62|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Maruhakayama Kofun'''|丸墓山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Maruhakayama Kofun is a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}) ''kofun'' with a diameter of 105 meters and height of 18.9 meters, and is thus one of the largest circular tumuli in Japan.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> [[Archaeological excavation]]s have not been conducted and details of the [[burial chamber]] are unknown. The surface of the tumulus appears to have been covered only in ''[[fukiishi]]'', with no ''[[haniwa]]'' shards having been discovered, and from this it is estimated that the tumulus was built in the early 6th century AD. From 1985 to 1986, maintenance work was conducted on the top and east side of the mound, and on a portion of the moat.
In 1590, during [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s [[Siege of Odawara (1590)|campaign against the Odawara Hōjō]], [[Ishida Mitsunari]] established his field headquarters on top of this tumulus, constructing a semi-circular embankment as part of the [[Siege of Oshi]] Castle, which is just visible from the top of the mound.
<gallery widths="140px"> Maruhakayama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|Panoramic view Gyoda Ishida Bank In Sakitama Kofun Park 1.JPG|Ishida Embankment Gyoda Maruhakayama Tumulus In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sakitama Fire Festival Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival in 2018 </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Maruhakayama Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Maruhakayama Kofun]][[Category:Maruhakayama Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Gyōda, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Maruhakayama Kofun | native_name = 丸墓山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Maruhakayama Tumulus In Spring from close.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|45.51|N|139|28|42.62|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Maruhakayama Kofun'''|丸墓山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Maruhakayama Kofun is a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}) ''kofun'' with a diameter of 105 meters and height of 18.9 meters, and is thus one of the largest circular tumuli in Japan.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> [[Archaeological excavation]]s have not been conducted and details of the [[burial chamber]] are unknown. The surface of the tumulus appears to have been covered only in ''[[fukiishi]]'', with no ''[[haniwa]]'' shards having been discovered, and from this it is estimated that the tumulus was built in the early 6th century AD. From 1985 to 1986, maintenance work was conducted on the top and east side of the mound, and on a portion of the moat.
In 1590, during [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s [[Siege of Odawara (1590)|campaign against the Odawara Hōjō]], [[Ishida Mitsunari]] established his field headquarters on top of this tumulus, constructing a semi-circular embankment as part of the [[Siege of Oshi]] Castle, which is just visible from the top of the mound.
<gallery widths="140px"> Maruhakayama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|Panoramic view Gyoda Ishida Bank In Sakitama Kofun Park 1.JPG|Ishida Embankment Gyoda Maruhakayama Tumulus In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sakitama Fire Festival Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival in 2018 </gallery>

In 1590, during [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s [[Siege of Odawara (1590)|campaign against the Odawara Hōjō]], [[Ishida Mitsunari]] established his field headquarters on top of this tumulus, constructing a semi-circular embankment as part of the [[Siege of Oshi]] Castle, which is just visible from the top of the mound.

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Gyōda, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Maruhakayama Kofun | native_name = 丸墓山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Maruhakayama Tumulus In Spring from close.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|45.51|N|139|28|42.62|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Maruhakayama Kofun'''|丸墓山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Maruhakayama Kofun is a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}) ''kofun'' with a diameter of 105 meters and height of 18.9 meters, and is thus one of the largest circular tumuli in Japan.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> [[Archaeological excavation]]s have not been conducted and details of the [[burial chamber]] are unknown. The surface of the tumulus appears to have been covered only in ''[[fukiishi]]'', with no ''[[haniwa]]'' shards having been discovered, and from this it is estimated that the tumulus was built in the early 6th century AD. From 1985 to 1986, maintenance work was conducted on the top and east side of the mound, and on a portion of the moat.
In 1590, during [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s [[Siege of Odawara (1590)|campaign against the Odawara Hōjō]], [[Ishida Mitsunari]] established his field headquarters on top of this tumulus, constructing a semi-circular embankment as part of the [[Siege of Oshi]] Castle, which is just visible from the top of the mound.
<gallery widths="140px"> Maruhakayama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|Panoramic view Gyoda Ishida Bank In Sakitama Kofun Park 1.JPG|Ishida Embankment Gyoda Maruhakayama Tumulus In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sakitama Fire Festival Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival in 2018 </gallery>

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Gyōda, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Maruhakayama Kofun | native_name = 丸墓山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Maruhakayama Tumulus In Spring from close.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|45.51|N|139|28|42.62|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Maruhakayama Kofun'''|丸墓山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Maruhakayama Kofun is a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with a diameter of 105 meters and height of 18.9 meters, and is thus one of the largest circular tumuli in Japan.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> [[Archaeological excavation]]s have not been conducted and details of the [[burial chamber]] are unknown. The surface of the tumulus appears to have been covered only in ''[[fukiishi]]'', with no ''[[haniwa]]'' shards having been discovered, and from this it is estimated that the tumulus was built in the early 6th century AD. From 1985 to 1986, maintenance work was conducted on the top and east side of the mound, and on a portion of the moat.
In 1590, during [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s [[Siege of Odawara (1590)|campaign against the Odawara Hōjō]], [[Ishida Mitsunari]] established his field headquarters on top of this tumulus, constructing a semi-circular embankment as part of the [[Siege of Oshi]] Castle, which is just visible from the top of the mound.
<gallery widths="140px"> Maruhakayama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|Panoramic view Gyoda Ishida Bank In Sakitama Kofun Park 1.JPG|Ishida Embankment Gyoda Maruhakayama Tumulus In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sakitama Fire Festival Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival in 2018 </gallery>

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Gyōda, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Maruhakayama Kofun | native_name = 丸墓山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Maruhakayama Tumulus In Spring from close.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|45.51|N|139|28|42.62|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Maruhakayama Kofun'''|丸墓山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Maruhakayama Kofun is a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with a diameter of 105 meters and height of 18.9 meters, and is thus one of the largest circular tumuli in Japan.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> [[Archaeological excavation]]s have not been conducted and details of the [[burial chamber]] are unknown. The surface of the tumulus appears to have been covered only in ''[[fukiishi]]'', with no ''[[haniwa]]'' shards having been discovered, and from this it is estimated that the tumulus was built in the early 6th century AD. From 1985 to 1986, maintenance work was conducted on the top and east side of the mound, and on a portion of the moat.
In 1590, during [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s [[Siege of Odawara (1590)|campaign against the Odawara Hōjō]], [[Ishida Mitsunari]] established his field headquarters on top of this tumulus, constructing a semi-circular embankment as part of the [[Siege of Oshi]] Castle, which is just visible from the top of the mound.
<gallery widths="140px"> Maruhakayama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|Panoramic view Gyoda Ishida Bank In Sakitama Kofun Park 1.JPG|Ishida Embankment Gyoda Maruhakayama Tumulus In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sakitama Fire Festival Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival in 2018 </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Gyōda, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Maruhakayama Kofun | native_name = 丸墓山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Maruhakayama Tumulus In Spring from close.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|45.51|N|139|28|42.62|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Maruhakayama Kofun'''|丸墓山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>

The surface of the tumulus appears to have been covered only in ''[[fukiishi]]'', with no ''[[haniwa]]'' shards having been discovered, and from this it is estimated that the tumulus was built in the early 6th century AD. From 1985 to 1986, maintenance work was conducted on the top and east side of the mound, and on a portion of the moat.
In 1590, during [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s [[Siege of Odawara (1590)|campaign against the Odawara Hōjō]], [[Ishida Mitsunari]] established his field headquarters on top of this tumulus, constructing a semi-circular embankment as part of the [[Siege of Oshi]] Castle, which is just visible from the top of the mound.

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Gyōda, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Maruhakayama Kofun | native_name = 丸墓山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Maruhakayama Tumulus In Spring from close.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|45.51|N|139|28|42.62|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Maruhakayama Kofun'''|丸墓山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Maruhakayama Kofun is a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with a diameter of 105 meters and height of 18.9 meters, and is thus one of the largest circular tumuli in Japan.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> [[Archaeological excavation]]s have not been conducted and details of the [[burial chamber]] are unknown. The surface of the tumulus appears to have been covered only in ''[[fukiishi]]'', with no ''[[haniwa]]'' shards having been discovered, and from this it is estimated that the tumulus was built in the early 6th century AD. From 1985 to 1986, maintenance work was conducted on the top and east side of the mound, and on a portion of the moat.
In 1590, during [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s [[Siege of Odawara (1590)|campaign against the Odawara Hōjō]], [[Ishida Mitsunari]] established his field headquarters on top of this tumulus, constructing a semi-circular embankment as part of the [[Siege of Oshi]] Castle, which is just visible from the top of the mound.
<gallery widths="140px"> Maruhakayama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|Panoramic view Gyoda Ishida Bank In Sakitama Kofun Park 1.JPG|Ishida Embankment Gyoda Maruhakayama Tumulus In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sakitama Fire Festival Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival in 2018 </gallery>

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Gyōda, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Maruhakayama Kofun | native_name = 丸墓山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Maruhakayama Tumulus In Spring from close.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|45.51|N|139|28|42.62|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Maruhakayama Kofun'''|丸墓山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Maruhakayama Kofun is a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with a diameter of 105 meters and height of 18.9 meters, and is thus one of the largest circular tumuli in Japan.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> [[Archaeological excavation]]s have not been conducted and details of the [[burial chamber]] are unknown. The surface of the tumulus appears to have been covered only in ''[[fukiishi]]'', with no ''[[haniwa]]'' shards having been discovered, and from this it is estimated that the tumulus was built in the early 6th century AD. From 1985 to 1986, maintenance work was conducted on the top and east side of the mound, and on a portion of the moat.
In 1590, during [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s [[Siege of Odawara (1590)|campaign against the Odawara Hōjō]], [[Ishida Mitsunari]] established his field headquarters on top of this tumulus, constructing a semi-circular embankment as part of the [[Siege of Oshi]] Castle, which is just visible from the top of the mound.
<gallery widths="140px"> Maruhakayama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|Panoramic view Gyoda Ishida Bank In Sakitama Kofun Park 1.JPG|Ishida Embankment Gyoda Maruhakayama Tumulus In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sakitama Fire Festival Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival in 2018 </gallery>

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Gyōda, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Maruhakayama Kofun | native_name = 丸墓山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Maruhakayama Tumulus In Spring from close.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|45.51|N|139|28|42.62|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Maruhakayama Kofun'''|丸墓山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Maruhakayama Kofun is a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with a diameter of 105 meters and height of 18.9 meters, and is thus one of the largest circular tumuli in Japan.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> [[Archaeological excavation]]s have not been conducted and details of the [[burial chamber]] are unknown. The surface of the tumulus appears to have been covered only in ''[[fukiishi]]'', with no ''[[haniwa]]'' shards having been discovered, and from this it is estimated that the tumulus was built in the early 6th century AD. From 1985 to 1986, maintenance work was conducted on the top and east side of the mound, and on a portion of the moat.
In 1590, during [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s [[Siege of Odawara (1590)|campaign against the Odawara Hōjō]], [[Ishida Mitsunari]] established his field headquarters on top of this tumulus, constructing a semi-circular embankment as part of the [[Siege of Oshi]] Castle, which is just visible from the top of the mound.
<gallery widths="140px"> Maruhakayama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|Panoramic view Gyoda Ishida Bank In Sakitama Kofun Park 1.JPG|Ishida Embankment Gyoda Maruhakayama Tumulus In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sakitama Fire Festival Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival in 2018 </gallery>

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Gyōda, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Maruhakayama Kofun | native_name = 丸墓山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Maruhakayama Tumulus In Spring from close.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|45.51|N|139|28|42.62|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Maruhakayama Kofun'''|丸墓山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Maruhakayama Kofun is a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with a diameter of 105 meters and height of 18.9 meters, and is thus one of the largest circular tumuli in Japan.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> [[Archaeological excavation]]s have not been conducted and details of the [[burial chamber]] are unknown. The surface of the tumulus appears to have been covered only in ''[[fukiishi]]'', with no ''[[haniwa]]'' shards having been discovered, and from this it is estimated that the tumulus was built in the early 6th century AD. From 1985 to 1986, maintenance work was conducted on the top and east side of the mound, and on a portion of the moat.
In 1590, during [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s [[Siege of Odawara (1590)|campaign against the Odawara Hōjō]], [[Ishida Mitsunari]] established his field headquarters on top of this tumulus, constructing a semi-circular embankment as part of the [[Siege of Oshi]] Castle, which is just visible from the top of the mound.
<gallery widths="140px"> Maruhakayama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|Panoramic view Gyoda Ishida Bank In Sakitama Kofun Park 1.JPG|Ishida Embankment Gyoda Maruhakayama Tumulus In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sakitama Fire Festival Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival in 2018 </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Gyōda, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Maruhakayama Kofun | native_name = 丸墓山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Maruhakayama Tumulus In Spring from close.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|45.51|N|139|28|42.62|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Maruhakayama Kofun'''|丸墓山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Maruhakayama Kofun is a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with a diameter of 105 meters and height of 18.9 meters, and is thus one of the largest circular tumuli in Japan.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> [[Archaeological excavation]]s have not been conducted and details of the [[burial chamber]] are unknown. The surface of the tumulus appears to have been covered only in ''[[fukiishi]]'', with no ''[[haniwa]]'' shards having been discovered, and from this it is estimated that the tumulus was built in the early 6th century AD. From 1985 to 1986, maintenance work was conducted on the top and east side of the mound, and on a portion of the moat.
In 1590, during [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s [[Siege of Odawara (1590)|campaign against the Odawara Hōjō]], [[Ishida Mitsunari]] established his field headquarters on top of this tumulus, constructing a semi-circular embankment as part of the [[Siege of Oshi]] Castle, which is just visible from the top of the mound.
<gallery widths="140px"> Maruhakayama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|Panoramic view Gyoda Ishida Bank In Sakitama Kofun Park 1.JPG|Ishida Embankment Gyoda Maruhakayama Tumulus In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sakitama Fire Festival Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival in 2018 </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Gyōda, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Maruhakayama Kofun | native_name = 丸墓山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Spring from close.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|45.51|N|139|28|42.62|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Maruhakayama Kofun'''|丸墓山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Maruhakayama Kofun is a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with a diameter of 105 meters and height of 18.9 meters, and is thus one of the largest circular tumuli in Japan.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> [[Archaeological excavation]]s have not been conducted and details of the [[burial chamber]] are unknown. The surface of the tumulus appears to have been covered only in ''[[fukiishi]]'', with no ''[[haniwa]]'' shards having been discovered, and from this it is estimated that the tumulus was built in the early 6th century AD. From 1985 to 1986, maintenance work was conducted on the top and east side of the mound, and on a portion of the moat.
In 1590, during [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s [[Siege of Odawara (1590)|campaign against the Odawara Hōjō]], [[Ishida Mitsunari]] established his field headquarters on top of this tumulus, constructing a semi-circular embankment as part of the [[Siege of Oshi]] Castle, which is just visible from the top of the mound.
<gallery widths="140px"> Maruhakayama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|Panoramic view Gyoda Ishida Bank In Sakitama Kofun Park 1.JPG|Ishida Embankment Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sakitama Fire Festival Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival in 2018 </gallery>

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Gyōda, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Maruhakayama Kofun | native_name = 丸墓山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Spring from close.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|45.51|N|139|28|42.62|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Maruhakayama Kofun'''|丸墓山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Maruhakayama Kofun is a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with a diameter of 105 meters and height of 18.9 meters, and is thus one of the largest circular tumuli in Japan.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> [[Archaeological excavation]]s have not been conducted and details of the [[burial chamber]] are unknown. The surface of the tumulus appears to have been covered only in ''[[fukiishi]]'', with no ''[[haniwa]]'' shards having been discovered, and from this it is estimated that the tumulus was built in the early 6th century AD. From 1985 to 1986, maintenance work was conducted on the top and east side of the mound, and on a portion of the moat.
In 1590, during [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s [[Siege of Odawara (1590)|campaign against the Odawara Hōjō]], [[Ishida Mitsunari]] established his field headquarters on top of this tumulus, constructing a semi-circular embankment as part of the [[Siege of Oshi]] Castle, which is just visible from the top of the mound.
<gallery widths="140px"> Maruhakayama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|Panoramic view Gyoda Ishida Bank In Sakitama Kofun Park 1.JPG|Ishida Embankment Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sakitama Fire Festival Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival in 2018 </gallery>

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Gyōda, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Maruhakayama Kofun | native_name = 丸墓山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Spring from close.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|45.51|N|139|28|42.62|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Maruhakayama Kofun'''|丸墓山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Maruhakayama Kofun is a {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with a diameter of 105 meters and height of 18.9 meters, and is thus one of the largest circular tumuli in Japan.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> [[Archaeological excavation]]s have not been conducted and details of the [[burial chamber]] are unknown. The surface of the tumulus appears to have been covered only in ''[[fukiishi]]'', with no ''[[haniwa]]'' shards having been discovered, and from this it is estimated that the tumulus was built in the early 6th century AD. From 1985 to 1986, maintenance work was conducted on the top and east side of the mound, and on a portion of the moat.
In 1590, during [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s [[Siege of Odawara (1590)|campaign against the Odawara Hōjō]], [[Ishida Mitsunari]] established his field headquarters on top of this tumulus, constructing a semi-circular embankment as part of the [[Siege of Oshi]] Castle, which is just visible from the top of the mound.
<gallery widths="140px"> Maruhakayama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|Panoramic view Gyoda Ishida Bank In Sakitama Kofun Park 1.JPG|Ishida Embankment Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sakitama Fire Festival Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival in 2018 </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Gyōda, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Maruhakayama Kofun | native_name = 丸墓山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Spring from close.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|45.51|N|139|28|42.62|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Maruhakayama Kofun'''|丸墓山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Maruhakayama Kofun is a {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with a diameter of 105 meters and height of 18.9 meters, and is thus one of the largest circular tumuli in Japan.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> [[Archaeological excavation]]s have not been conducted and details of the [[burial chamber]] are unknown. The surface of the tumulus appears to have been covered only in ''[[fukiishi]]'', with no ''[[haniwa]]'' shards having been discovered, and from this it is estimated that the tumulus was built in the early 6th century AD. From 1985 to 1986, maintenance work was conducted on the top and east side of the mound, and on a portion of the moat.
In 1590, during [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s [[Siege of Odawara (1590)|campaign against the Odawara Hōjō]], [[Ishida Mitsunari]] established his field headquarters on top of this tumulus, constructing a semi-circular embankment as part of the [[Siege of Oshi]] Castle, which is just visible from the top of the mound.
<gallery widths="140px"> Maruhakayama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|Panoramic view Gyoda Ishida Bank In Sakitama Kofun Park 1.JPG|Ishida Embankment Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sakitama Fire Festival Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival in 2018 </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Gyōda, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Maruhakayama Kofun | native_name = 丸墓山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Spring from close.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|45.51|N|139|28|42.62|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Maruhakayama Kofun'''|丸墓山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Maruhakayama Kofun is a {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with a diameter of 105 meters and height of 18.9 meters, and is thus one of the largest circular tumuli in Japan.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> [[Archaeological excavation]]s have not been conducted and details of the [[burial chamber]] are unknown. The surface of the tumulus appears to have been covered only in ''[[fukiishi]]'', with no ''[[haniwa]]'' shards having been discovered, and from this it is estimated that the tumulus was built in the early 6th century AD. From 1985 to 1986, maintenance work was conducted on the top and east side of the mound, and on a portion of the moat.
In 1590, during [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s [[Siege of Odawara (1590)|campaign against the Odawara Hōjō]], [[Ishida Mitsunari]] established his field headquarters on top of this tumulus, constructing a semi-circular embankment as part of the [[Siege of Oshi]] Castle, which is just visible from the top of the mound.
<gallery widths="140px"> Maruhakayama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|Panoramic view Gyoda Ishida Bank In Sakitama Kofun Park 1.JPG|Ishida Embankment Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sakitama Fire Festival Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival in 2018 </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]

The surface of the tumulus appears to have been covered only in ''[[fukiishi]]'', with no ''[[haniwa]]'' shards having been discovered, and from this it is estimated that the tumulus was built in the early 6th century AD. From 1985 to 1986, maintenance work was conducted on the top and east side of the mound, and on a portion of the moat.
In 1590, during [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s [[Siege of Odawara (1590)|campaign against the Odawara Hōjō]], [[Ishida Mitsunari]] established his field headquarters on top of this tumulus, constructing a semi-circular embankment as part of the [[Siege of Oshi]] Castle, which is just visible from the top of the mound.
