{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Gyōda, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Maruhakayama Kofun | native_name = 丸墓山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Spring from close.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|45.51|N|139|28|42.62|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Maruhakayama Kofun'''|丸墓山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Maruhakayama Kofun is a {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with a diameter of 105 meters and height of 18.9 meters, and is thus one of the largest circular tumuli in Japan.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> [[Archaeological excavation]]s have not been conducted and details of the [[burial chamber]] are unknown. The surface of the tumulus appears to have been covered only in ''[[fukiishi]]'', with no ''[[haniwa]]'' shards having been discovered, and from this it is estimated that the tumulus was built in the early 6th century AD. From 1985 to 1986, maintenance work was conducted on the top and east side of the mound, and on a portion of the moat.
In 1590, during [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s [[Siege of Odawara (1590)|campaign against the Odawara Hōjō]], [[Ishida Mitsunari]] established his field headquarters on top of this tumulus, constructing a semi-circular embankment as part of the [[Siege of Oshi]] Castle, which is just visible from the top of the mound.
<gallery widths="140px"> Maruhakayama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|Panoramic view Gyoda Ishida Bank In Sakitama Kofun Park 1.JPG|Ishida Embankment Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sakitama Fire Festival Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival in 2018 </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]

From 1985 to 1986, maintenance work was conducted on the top and east side of the mound, and on a portion of the moat.
In 1590, during [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s [[Siege of Odawara (1590)|campaign against the Odawara Hōjō]], [[Ishida Mitsunari]] established his field headquarters on top of this tumulus, constructing a semi-circular embankment as part of the [[Siege of Oshi]] Castle, which is just visible from the top of the mound.
<gallery widths="140px"> Maruhakayama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|Panoramic view Gyoda Ishida Bank In Sakitama Kofun Park 1.JPG|Ishida Embankment Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sakitama Fire Festival Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival in 2018 </gallery>

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Gyōda, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Maruhakayama Kofun | native_name = 丸墓山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Spring from close.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|45.51|N|139|28|42.62|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Maruhakayama Kofun'''|丸墓山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Maruhakayama Kofun is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with a diameter of 105 meters and height of 18.9 meters, and is thus one of the largest circular tumuli in Japan.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> [[Archaeological excavation]]s have not been conducted and details of the [[burial chamber]] are unknown. The surface of the tumulus appears to have been covered only in ''[[fukiishi]]'', with no ''[[haniwa]]'' shards having been discovered, and from this it is estimated that the tumulus was built in the early 6th century AD. From 1985 to 1986, maintenance work was conducted on the top and east side of the mound, and on a portion of the moat.
In 1590, during [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s [[Siege of Odawara (1590)|campaign against the Odawara Hōjō]], [[Ishida Mitsunari]] established his field headquarters on top of this tumulus, constructing a semi-circular embankment as part of the [[Siege of Oshi]] Castle, which is just visible from the top of the mound.
<gallery widths="140px"> Maruhakayama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|Panoramic view Gyoda Ishida Bank In Sakitama Kofun Park 1.JPG|Ishida Embankment Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sakitama Fire Festival Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival in 2018 </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline}} *[https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/15/04/12/map/tetu.html Gyoda city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/埼玉古墳群紹介/丸墓山古墳 Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns] {{in lang|ja}}

In 1590, during [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s [[Siege of Odawara (1590)|campaign against the Odawara Hōjō]], [[Ishida Mitsunari]] established his field headquarters on top of this tumulus, constructing a semi-circular embankment as part of the [[Siege of Oshi]] Castle, which is just visible from the top of the mound.
<gallery widths="140px"> Maruhakayama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|Panoramic view Gyoda Ishida Bank In Sakitama Kofun Park 1.JPG|Ishida Embankment Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sakitama Fire Festival Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival in 2018 </gallery>

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Gyōda, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Maruhakayama Kofun | native_name = 丸墓山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Spring from close.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|45.51|N|139|28|42.62|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda, Saitama]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Maruhakayama Kofun'''|丸墓山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the city of [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Maruhakayama Kofun is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with a diameter of 105 meters and height of 18.9 meters, and is thus one of the largest circular tumuli in Japan.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> [[Archaeological excavation]]s have not been conducted and details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are unknown. The surface of the tumulus appears to have been covered only in ''[[fukiishi]]'', with no ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' shards having been discovered, and from this it is estimated that the tumulus was built in the early 6th century AD. From 1985 to 1986, maintenance work was conducted on the top and east side of the mound, and on a portion of the moat.
In 1590, during [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s [[Siege of Odawara (1590)|campaign against the Odawara Hōjō]], [[Ishida Mitsunari]] established his field headquarters on top of this tumulus, constructing a semi-circular embankment as part of the [[Siege of Oshi]] Castle, which is just visible from the top of the mound.
<gallery widths="140px"> Maruhakayama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|Panoramic view Gyoda Ishida Bank In Sakitama Kofun Park 1.JPG|Ishida Embankment Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sakitama Fire Festival Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival in 2018 </gallery>

In 1590, during [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s [[Siege of Odawara (1590)|campaign against the Odawara Hōjō]], [[Ishida Mitsunari]] established his field headquarters on top of this tumulus, constructing a semi-circular embankment as part of the [[Siege of Oshi]] Castle, which is just visible from the top of the mound.
<gallery widths="140px"> Maruhakayama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|Panoramic view Gyoda Ishida Bank In Sakitama Kofun Park 1.JPG|Ishida Embankment Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sakitama Fire Festival Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival in 2018 </gallery>

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Gyōda, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Maruhakayama Kofun | native_name = 丸墓山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Spring from close.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|45.51|N|139|28|42.62|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda, Saitama]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Maruhakayama Kofun'''|丸墓山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the city of [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Maruhakayama Kofun is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with a diameter of 105 meters and height of 18.9 meters, and is thus one of the largest circular tumuli in Japan.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> [[Archaeological excavation]]s have not been conducted and details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are unknown. The surface of the tumulus appears to have been covered only in ''[[fukiishi]]'', with no ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' shards having been discovered, and from this it is estimated that the tumulus was built in the early 6th century AD. From 1985 to 1986, maintenance work was conducted on the top and east side of the mound, and on a portion of the moat.
In 1590, during [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s [[Siege of Odawara (1590)|campaign against the Odawara Hōjō]], [[Ishida Mitsunari]] established his field headquarters on top of this tumulus, constructing a semi-circular embankment as part of the [[Siege of Oshi]] Castle, which is just visible from the top of the mound.
<gallery widths="140px"> Maruhakayama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|Panoramic view Gyoda Ishida Bank In Sakitama Kofun Park 1.JPG|Ishida Embankment Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sakitama Fire Festival Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival in 2018 </gallery>

The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Maruhakayama Kofun is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with a diameter of 105 meters and height of 18.9 meters, and is thus one of the largest circular tumuli in Japan.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> [[Archaeological excavation]]s have not been conducted and details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are unknown. The surface of the tumulus appears to have been covered only in ''[[fukiishi]]'', with no ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' shards having been discovered, and from this it is estimated that the tumulus was built in the early 6th century AD. From 1985 to 1986, maintenance work was conducted on the top and east side of the mound, and on a portion of the moat.
In 1590, during [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s [[:en:Siege of Odawara (1590)|campaign against the Odawara Hōjō]], [[Ishida Mitsunari]] established his field headquarters on top of this tumulus, constructing a semi-circular embankment as part of the [[:en:Siege of Oshi|Siege of Oshi]] Castle, which is just visible from the top of the mound.
<gallery widths="140px"> Maruhakayama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|Panoramic view Gyoda Ishida Bank In Sakitama Kofun Park 1.JPG|Ishida Embankment Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sakitama Fire Festival Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival in 2018 </gallery>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline}} *[https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/15/04/12/map/tetu.html Gyoda city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/埼玉古墳群紹介/丸墓山古墳 Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns] {{in lang|ja}}

==Overview== The Maruhakayama Kofun is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with a diameter of 105 meters and height of 18.9 meters, and is thus one of the largest circular tumuli in Japan.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> [[Archaeological excavation]]s have not been conducted and details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are unknown. The surface of the tumulus appears to have been covered only in ''[[fukiishi]]'', with no ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' shards having been discovered, and from this it is estimated that the tumulus was built in the early 6th century AD. From 1985 to 1986, maintenance work was conducted on the top and east side of the mound, and on a portion of the moat.
In 1590, during [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s [[:en:Siege of Odawara (1590)|campaign against the Odawara Hōjō]], [[Ishida Mitsunari]] established his field headquarters on top of this tumulus, constructing a semi-circular embankment as part of the [[:en:Siege of Oshi|Siege of Oshi]] Castle, which is just visible from the top of the mound.
<gallery widths="140px"> Maruhakayama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|Panoramic view Gyoda Ishida Bank In Sakitama Kofun Park 1.JPG|Ishida Embankment Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sakitama Fire Festival Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival in 2018 </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Maruhakayama Kofun]][[en:Maruhakayama Kofun]][[ja:丸墓山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11368078]] Wikidata name: Maruhakayama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Gyōda, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Maruhakayama Kofun | native_name = 丸墓山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Tumulus In Spring from close.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.129308333333|N|139.47850555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Gyoda|WD=Q860659}} | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of ={{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|WD=Q2213580}} | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Maruhakayama Kofun'''|丸墓山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the city of [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Maruhakayama Kofun is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with a diameter of 105 meters and height of 18.9 meters, and is thus one of the largest circular tumuli in Japan.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> [[Archaeological excavation]]s have not been conducted and details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are unknown. The surface of the tumulus appears to have been covered only in ''[[fukiishi]]'', with no ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' shards having been discovered, and from this it is estimated that the tumulus was built in the early 6th century AD. From 1985 to 1986, maintenance work was conducted on the top and east side of the mound, and on a portion of the moat.
In 1590, during [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s [[:en:Siege of Odawara (1590)|campaign against the Odawara Hōjō]], [[Ishida Mitsunari]] established his field headquarters on top of this tumulus, constructing a semi-circular embankment as part of the [[:en:Siege of Oshi|Siege of Oshi]] Castle, which is just visible from the top of the mound.
<gallery widths="140px"> Maruhakayama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|Panoramic view Gyoda Ishida Bank In Sakitama Kofun Park 1.JPG|Ishida Embankment Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sakitama Fire Festival Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival in 2018 </gallery>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline}} *[https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/15/04/12/map/tetu.html Gyoda city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/埼玉古墳群紹介/丸墓山古墳 Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns] {{in lang|ja}}

The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Maruhakayama Kofun is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with a diameter of 105 meters and height of 18.9 meters, and is thus one of the largest circular tumuli in Japan.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> [[Archaeological excavation]]s have not been conducted and details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are unknown. The surface of the tumulus appears to have been covered only in ''[[fukiishi]]'', with no ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' shards having been discovered, and from this it is estimated that the tumulus was built in the early 6th century AD. From 1985 to 1986, maintenance work was conducted on the top and east side of the mound, and on a portion of the moat.
In 1590, during [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s [[:en:Siege of Odawara (1590)|campaign against the Odawara Hōjō]], [[Ishida Mitsunari]] established his field headquarters on top of this tumulus, constructing a semi-circular embankment as part of the [[:en:Siege of Oshi|Siege of Oshi]] Castle, which is just visible from the top of the mound.
<gallery widths="140px"> Maruhakayama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|Panoramic view Gyoda Ishida Bank In Sakitama Kofun Park 1.JPG|Ishida Embankment Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sakitama Fire Festival Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival in 2018 </gallery>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline}} *[https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/15/04/12/map/tetu.html Gyoda city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/埼玉古墳群紹介/丸墓山古墳 Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns] {{in lang|ja}}

Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Maruhakayama Kofun is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with a diameter of 105 meters and height of 18.9 meters, and is thus one of the largest circular tumuli in Japan.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> [[Archaeological excavation]]s have not been conducted and details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are unknown. The surface of the tumulus appears to have been covered only in ''[[fukiishi]]'', with no ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' shards having been discovered, and from this it is estimated that the tumulus was built in the early 6th century AD. From 1985 to 1986, maintenance work was conducted on the top and east side of the mound, and on a portion of the moat.
In 1590, during [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s [[:en:Siege of Odawara (1590)|campaign against the Odawara Hōjō]], [[Ishida Mitsunari]] established his field headquarters on top of this tumulus, constructing a semi-circular embankment as part of the [[:en:Siege of Oshi|Siege of Oshi]] Castle, which is just visible from the top of the mound.
<gallery widths="140px"> Maruhakayama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|Panoramic view Gyoda Ishida Bank In Sakitama Kofun Park 1.JPG|Ishida Embankment Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sakitama Fire Festival Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival in 2018 </gallery>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]

The surface of the tumulus appears to have been covered only in ''[[fukiishi]]'', with no ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' shards having been discovered, and from this it is estimated that the tumulus was built in the early 6th century AD. From 1985 to 1986, maintenance work was conducted on the top and east side of the mound, and on a portion of the moat.
In 1590, during [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s [[:en:Siege of Odawara (1590)|campaign against the Odawara Hōjō]], [[Ishida Mitsunari]] established his field headquarters on top of this tumulus, constructing a semi-circular embankment as part of the [[:en:Siege of Oshi|Siege of Oshi]] Castle, which is just visible from the top of the mound.
<gallery widths="140px"> Maruhakayama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|Panoramic view Gyoda Ishida Bank In Sakitama Kofun Park 1.JPG|Ishida Embankment Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sakitama Fire Festival Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival in 2018 </gallery>

In 1590, during {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}'s {{ill|Siege of Odawara (1590)|en|Siege of Odawara (1590)|WD=Q2622890|lt=campaign against the Odawara Hōjō}}, [[Ishida Mitsunari]] established his field headquarters on top of this tumulus, constructing a semi-circular embankment as part of the {{ill|Siege of Oshi|en|Siege of Oshi|WD=Q15663170|lt=Siege of Oshi}} Castle, which is just visible from the top of the mound.
<gallery widths="140px"> Maruhakayama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|Panoramic view Gyoda Ishida Bank In Sakitama Kofun Park 1.JPG|Ishida Embankment Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sakitama Fire Festival Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival in 2018 </gallery>

Saitama|Gyōda]], {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Maruhakayama Kofun is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with a diameter of 105 meters and height of 18.9 meters, and is thus one of the largest circular tumuli in Japan.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> [[Archaeological excavation]]s have not been conducted and details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are unknown. The surface of the tumulus appears to have been covered only in ''[[fukiishi]]'', with no ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' shards having been discovered, and from this it is estimated that the tumulus was built in the early 6th century AD. From 1985 to 1986, maintenance work was conducted on the top and east side of the mound, and on a portion of the moat.
In 1590, during {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}'s {{ill|Siege of Odawara (1590)|en|Siege of Odawara (1590)|WD=Q2622890|lt=campaign against the Odawara Hōjō}}, [[Ishida Mitsunari]] established his field headquarters on top of this tumulus, constructing a semi-circular embankment as part of the {{ill|Siege of Oshi|en|Siege of Oshi|WD=Q15663170|lt=Siege of Oshi}} Castle, which is just visible from the top of the mound.
<gallery widths="140px"> Maruhakayama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|Panoramic view Gyoda Ishida Bank In Sakitama Kofun Park 1.JPG|Ishida Embankment Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sakitama Fire Festival Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival in 2018 </gallery>

In 1590, during {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}'s {{ill|Siege of Odawara (1590)|en|Siege of Odawara (1590)|WD=Q2622890|lt=campaign against the Odawara Hōjō}}, [[Ishida Mitsunari]] established his field headquarters on top of this tumulus, constructing a semi-circular embankment as part of the {{ill|Siege of Oshi|en|Siege of Oshi|WD=Q15663170|lt=Siege of Oshi}} Castle, which is just visible from the top of the mound.
<gallery widths="140px"> Maruhakayama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|Panoramic view Gyoda Ishida Bank In Sakitama Kofun Park 1.JPG|Ishida Embankment Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sakitama Fire Festival Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival in 2018 </gallery>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Maruhakayama Kofun]][[en:Maruhakayama Kofun]][[ja:丸墓山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Maruhakayama Kofun]][[en:Maruhakayama Kofun]][[ja:丸墓山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Maruhakayama Kofun]][[en:Maruhakayama Kofun]][[ja:丸墓山古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Gyōda, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Maruhakayama Kofun | native_name = 丸墓山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Tumulus In Spring from close.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.129308333333|N|139.47850555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Gyoda|WD=Q860659}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of ={{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|WD=Q2213580}} | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Maruhakayama Kofun'''|丸墓山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the city of [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Maruhakayama Kofun is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with a diameter of 105 meters and height of 18.9 meters, and is thus one of the largest circular tumuli in Japan.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> [[Archaeological excavation]]s have not been conducted and details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are unknown. The surface of the tumulus appears to have been covered only in ''[[fukiishi]]'', with no ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' shards having been discovered, and from this it is estimated that the tumulus was built in the early 6th century AD. From 1985 to 1986, maintenance work was conducted on the top and east side of the mound, and on a portion of the moat.
In 1590, during {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}'s {{ill|Siege of Odawara (1590)|en|Siege of Odawara (1590)|WD=Q2622890|lt=campaign against the Odawara Hōjō}}, [[Ishida Mitsunari]] established his field headquarters on top of this tumulus, constructing a semi-circular embankment as part of the {{ill|Siege of Oshi|en|Siege of Oshi|WD=Q15663170|lt=Siege of Oshi}} Castle, which is just visible from the top of the mound.
<gallery widths="140px"> Maruhakayama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|Panoramic view Gyoda Ishida Bank In Sakitama Kofun Park 1.JPG|Ishida Embankment Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sakitama Fire Festival Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival in 2018 </gallery>

In 1590, during {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}'s {{ill|Siege of Odawara (1590)|en|Siege of Odawara (1590)|WD=Q2622890|lt=campaign against the Odawara Hōjō|1=Siege of Odawara (1590)}}, [[Ishida Mitsunari]] established his field headquarters on top of this tumulus, constructing a semi-circular embankment as part of the {{ill|Siege of Oshi|en|Siege of Oshi|WD=Q15663170|lt=Siege of Oshi|1=Siege of Oshi}} Castle, which is just visible from the top of the mound.
<gallery widths="140px"> Maruhakayama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|Panoramic view Gyoda Ishida Bank In Sakitama Kofun Park 1.JPG|Ishida Embankment Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sakitama Fire Festival Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival in 2018 </gallery>

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Gyōda, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Maruhakayama Kofun | native_name = 丸墓山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Tumulus In Spring from close.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maruhakayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.129308333333|N|139.47850555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Gyoda|WD=Q860659|1=Gyōda}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of ={{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|WD=Q2213580|1=Sakitama Kofun Cluster}} | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Maruhakayama Kofun'''|丸墓山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the city of [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Maruhakayama Kofun is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with a diameter of 105 meters and height of 18.9 meters, and is thus one of the largest circular tumuli in Japan.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> [[Archaeological excavation]]s have not been conducted and details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are unknown. The surface of the tumulus appears to have been covered only in ''[[fukiishi]]'', with no ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' shards having been discovered, and from this it is estimated that the tumulus was built in the early 6th century AD. From 1985 to 1986, maintenance work was conducted on the top and east side of the mound, and on a portion of the moat.
In 1590, during {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}'s {{ill|Siege of Odawara (1590)|en|Siege of Odawara (1590)|WD=Q2622890|lt=campaign against the Odawara Hōjō|1=Siege of Odawara (1590)}}, [[Ishida Mitsunari]] established his field headquarters on top of this tumulus, constructing a semi-circular embankment as part of the {{ill|Siege of Oshi|en|Siege of Oshi|WD=Q15663170|lt=Siege of Oshi|1=Siege of Oshi}} Castle, which is just visible from the top of the mound.
<gallery widths="140px"> Maruhakayama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|Panoramic view Gyoda Ishida Bank In Sakitama Kofun Park 1.JPG|Ishida Embankment Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sakitama Fire Festival Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival in 2018 </gallery>

The surface of the tumulus appears to have been covered only in ''[[fukiishi]]'', with no ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' shards having been discovered, and from this it is estimated that the tumulus was built in the early 6th century AD. From 1985 to 1986, maintenance work was conducted on the top and east side of the mound, and on a portion of the moat.
In 1590, during {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}'s {{ill|Siege of Odawara (1590)|en|Siege of Odawara (1590)|WD=Q2622890|lt=campaign against the Odawara Hōjō|1=Siege of Odawara (1590)}}, [[Ishida Mitsunari]] established his field headquarters on top of this tumulus, constructing a semi-circular embankment as part of the {{ill|Siege of Oshi|en|Siege of Oshi|WD=Q15663170|lt=Siege of Oshi|1=Siege of Oshi}} Castle, which is just visible from the top of the mound.
<gallery widths="140px"> Maruhakayama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|Panoramic view Gyoda Ishida Bank In Sakitama Kofun Park 1.JPG|Ishida Embankment Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sakitama Fire Festival Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival in 2018 </gallery>
